---
format:
  html:
    footnotes-hover: false
    css: styles.css
mainfont: "GT-America"
linkcolor: "#00355a"
anchor-sections: false
params:
  state: "AK"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<head>
<img src='https://csg-state-violent-crime.netlify.app/img/csgjc-logo.png' style='display: none;'> 
</head>

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(csgjcr)
library(highcharter)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(reactable)
 
source("R/utils.R")

# set path to data folder on sharepoint
sp_data_to_plot_path <- csg_sp_path("ad_hoc_requests/state_violent_crime_marshall/data")

state <- csg_state_convert(params$state, from = "abbr", "name")
state_name_text <- if_else(params$state == "DC", "the District of Columbia", state)

# read in crime data
srs_state <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds")) |> 
  filter(state_name == state, crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013)
srs_by_cat_state <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_by_cat_state.rds")) |> 
  filter(state_name == state, crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013)


srs_state_all <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, "srs_state.rds")) |> 
    filter(crime_cat == "Violent", year >= 2013)

srs_by_cat_us <- srs_state_all |>
  group_by(year, crime_cat, state_name = "United States", state_abbr = "U.S.") |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved),
    \(x) sum(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total / 4,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_us <- srs_state_all |>
  group_by(year, state_name = "United States", state_abbr = "U.S.", group) |>
  summarize(
    across(c(pop_total, incidents_reported, incidents_unsolved), sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    pop_total = pop_total,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported / pop_total,
    pct_unsolved = incidents_unsolved / incidents_reported
  )

srs_unsolved_by_county_to_map <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, 
                                             "viol_crime_unsolved_by_county_to_map_clean.rds")) |>
  filter(acs_county_state_name == state) 

srs_unsolved_by_agency_to_table <- read_rds(file.path(sp_data_to_plot_path, 
                                             "viol_crime_unsolved_by_agency_to_table.rds")) |>  
  filter(state_name == state)

### create object with states to exclude
states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz <- c("Illinois",
                                              "New York")

# create title of page
# we don't have IL or NY clearance rate data so take that out of title
doc_title <- case_when(
  state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz ~ paste(state, "data on violent crime"),
  state == "District of Columbia" ~ paste(state, "data on violent crime and arrests"),
  TRUE ~ paste(state, "data on violent crime and arrests"),
  )

### create dynamic text for 3 states with low reporting numbers
low_reporting_dynamic <- case_when(
  state == "Florida" ~ paste0("Note: Florida’s 2022 estimates were based on data from 67 percent fewer agencies and covered 33 percent less of the population compared to 2020."),
  state == "Illinois" ~ paste0("Note: Illinois’ 2022 estimates were based on data submitted from 32 percent fewer agencies and covered 16 percent less of the population compared to 2020."),
    state == "Hawaii" ~ paste0("Note: Hawaii’s 2022 estimates were based on data submitted by 25 percent fewer agencies and covered 12 percent less of the population compared to 2020."),
  TRUE ~ paste0(""),
  )

axis_expansion_mult <- 0.5
```

---
title: "`r doc_title`"
---

```{r}
min_max_viol_crime <- srs_by_cat_state |> 
  filter(year %in% range(year)) |> 
  arrange(year)

max_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[2]
min_year <- min_max_viol_crime$year[1] 

pct_change_crime <- (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[2] - min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[1]) / min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[1]

change_text_crime <- case_when(
  pct_change_crime > 0.005 &
    pct_change_crime < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  pct_change_crime >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  pct_change_crime < -0.005 &
    pct_change_crime > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  pct_change_crime <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_crime * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )

us_state_rate_comp <- (min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[2] - srs_by_cat_us[srs_by_cat_us$year==2023, ]$incidents_reported_rate_total) / srs_by_cat_us[srs_by_cat_us$year==2023, ]$incidents_reported_rate_total

us_state_rate_text_crime <- case_when(
  us_state_rate_comp > 0.005 &
    us_state_rate_comp < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  us_state_rate_comp >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent higher than"),
  us_state_rate_comp < -0.005 &
    us_state_rate_comp > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  us_state_rate_comp <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(us_state_rate_comp * 100), 1),
                                    " percent lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )


pct_change_unsolved <- min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[2] - min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[1]

change_text_clear <- case_when(
  pct_change_unsolved > 0.005 &
    pct_change_unsolved < 0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage point higher than"),
  pct_change_unsolved >= 0.015  ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage points higher than"),
  pct_change_unsolved < -0.005 &
    pct_change_unsolved > -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage point lower than"),
  pct_change_unsolved <= -0.015 ~ paste0(comma(abs(pct_change_unsolved * 100), 1),
                                    " percentage points lower than"),
  TRUE                      ~ "nearly the same as"
  )
```

## Violent crime in `r state_name_text`

```{r}
crime_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$incidents_reported_rate_total[2], 1, scale = 1e5))
```

In `r max_year`, `r crime_rate_max` violent crime incidents per 100,000 residents were reported to police in `r state_name_text`. This is `r change_text_crime` the violent crime rate in `r min_year`.

The crime rate in `r state_name_text` in 2023 was `r us_state_rate_text_crime` the United States average.

Data regarding crime and unsolved rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr). Violent crime includes homicide, aggravated assault, robbery, and rape. Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total population for a given geography. For more information, see the data sources and methodology section below.

`r low_reporting_dynamic`

::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_state |> 
  bind_rows(srs_by_cat_us) |> 
  mutate(
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5,
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Violent incidents per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = jr_pal[c(1, 7)],
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )    
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

```{r}
to_plot <- srs_state |> 
  bind_rows(srs_us) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  group_by(year, state_abbr) |>
  mutate(
    tot_crime = sum(incidents_reported),
    pct = incidents_reported / tot_crime,
    incidents_reported_rate_total = incidents_reported_rate_total * 1e5
    ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    group = fct_reorder(group, incidents_reported_rate_total),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Incidents reported per 100k: ", comma(incidents_reported_rate_total, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Total violent incidents reported: ", comma(tot_crime, 1), "<br>",
      "Share of all violent incidents: ", percent(pct, 1)
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    ) |> 
  arrange(year, group)
```

## Homicide

```{r}
to_plot_2 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Homicide")

rng <- range(to_plot_2$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_2 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_2$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_title(text = paste0("Homicide in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Homicide incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |>
  hc_setup()
```

## Aggravated assault

```{r}
to_plot_3 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Aggravated assault")

rng <- range(to_plot_3$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_3 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_3$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Aggravated assault in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where aggravated assault was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |>
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

## Robbery

```{r}
to_plot_4 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Robbery")

rng <- range(to_plot_4$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_4 |>
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_4$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Robbery in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where robbery was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |>   hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

## Rape

```{r}
to_plot_5 <- to_plot |> 
  filter(group == "Rape")

rng <- range(to_plot_5$incidents_reported_rate_total)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult

to_plot_5 |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = state_name),
    color = c(unique(to_plot_5$color), jr_pal[7]),
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),    
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Rape in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Incidents reported to police per 100,000 residents where rape was the most serious offense") |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE
  ) |>
  hc_setup()
```

## By offense

```{r}
to_plot |>
  filter(state_name == state) |> 
  hchart(
    "column",
    stacking = "normal",
    groupPadding = 0.05,
    hcaes(year, incidents_reported_rate_total, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, rate: {point.y:.1f}"
        )
      )      
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime in ", state_name_text, ", by offense")) |>
  hc_subtitle(text = "Crime reported to police per 100,000 residents") |> 
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program SRS state estimates") |> 
  hc_legend(reversed = "true") |> 
  hc_setup()
```

:::

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("## Unsolved violent crime in", state_name_text)
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz}
unsolved_rate_max <- paste0(comma(min_max_viol_crime$pct_unsolved[2] * 100, 1), " percent")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
  "In ", max_year, ", ", unsolved_rate_max, " of violent crimes reported to police in ", state_name_text, " were not solved. This is ", change_text_clear, " the unsolved rate in ", min_year, ".", '\n','\n',
    
    "Unsolved rates are the number of reported crimes not cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. For more information, see the data sources and methodology section below."
))
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("
::: {.panel-tabset}

## All violent crime
")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz}
to_plot <- srs_by_cat_state |> 
  bind_rows(srs_by_cat_us) |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  mutate(
    ### shouldn't be an issue for overall viz
    ### but just in case, recoding any unsolved rate below 0 to 0
    pct_unsolved_clean = ifelse(pct_unsolved<0 & !is.na(pct_unsolved),
                          0,pct_unsolved),
    pct_unsolved_clean = pct_unsolved_clean * 100,
    ### create pct_unsolved and incidents_unsolved for tooltip
    ### where if value is below 0, explanatory text is displayed
    ### instead of number
    pct_unsolved_tooltip = ifelse(pct_unsolved<0 & !is.na(pct_unsolved),
                                  "Less than 0%",as.character(percent(pct_unsolved_clean / 100, 1))),
    incidents_unsolved_tooltip = ifelse(incidents_unsolved<0 & !is.na(incidents_unsolved),
                                  "Fewer than 0",as.character(comma(incidents_unsolved))),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", state_name, " – ", year, "</b><br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", pct_unsolved_tooltip, "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", incidents_unsolved_tooltip
      ),
    state_name = fct_relevel(state_name, "United States", after = Inf)
    )

rng <- range(to_plot$pct_unsolved_clean)
mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult

y_min <- rng[1] - mult
y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
y_max <- rng[2] + mult
y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |> 
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_unsolved_clean, group = state_name),
    color = jr_pal[c(1, 7)],
    dashStyle = c("solid", "dash"),
    opacity = c(1, 0.75),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "year: {point.x:.0f}, unsolved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )     
  ) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste("Violent crime unsolved rate in ", state_name_text)) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program<br>Aggregated from SRS agency-level Offenses Known reports") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = y_min,
    max = y_max,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  )
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("
## By offense
")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz}
to_plot <- srs_state |> 
  filter(year != 2021) |> 
  left_join(offense_pal, by = c("group" = "crime")) |> 
  mutate(
    ### recode any unsolved rate below 0 to 0
    pct_unsolved_clean = ifelse(pct_unsolved<0 & !is.na(pct_unsolved),
                          0,pct_unsolved),
    pct_unsolved_clean = pct_unsolved_clean * 100,
    ### create pct_unsolved and incidents_unsolved for tooltip
    ### where if value is below 0, explanatory text is displayed
    ### instead of number
    pct_unsolved_tooltip = ifelse(pct_unsolved<0 & !is.na(pct_unsolved),
                                  "Less than 0%",as.character(percent(pct_unsolved_clean / 100, 1))),
    incidents_unsolved_tooltip = ifelse(incidents_unsolved<0 & !is.na(incidents_unsolved),
                                  "Fewer than 0",as.character(comma(round(incidents_unsolved,
                                                                          digits=0))))) |> 
  mutate(
    group = fct_reorder(group, pct_unsolved, last),
    tooltip = paste0(
      "<b>", group, " – ", year, "</b></br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", pct_unsolved_tooltip, "<br>",
      "Incidents reported: ", comma(incidents_reported, 1), "<br>",
      "Incidents unsolved: ", incidents_unsolved_tooltip
      )
    ) |> 
  arrange(desc(year), pct_unsolved_clean)

# rng <- range(to_plot$pct_unsolved_clean)
# mult <- diff(rng) * axis_expansion_mult
# 
# y_min <- rng[1] - mult
# y_min <- if_else(y_min < 0, 0, y_min)
# y_max <- rng[2] + mult
# y_max <- if_else(y_max > 100, 100, y_max)

to_plot |>   
  hchart(
    "spline",
    hcaes(year, pct_unsolved_clean, group = group),
    accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "{point.state}, year: {point.x:.0f}, unsolved rate: {point.y:.1f}%"
        )
      )       
  ) |> 
  hc_colors(unique(to_plot$color)) |> 
  hc_title(text = paste0("Violent crime unsolved rate in ", state_name_text, ", by offense")) |>
  hc_caption(text = "FBI Uniform Crime Reporting Program<br>Aggregated from SRS agency-level Offenses Known reports") |> 
  hc_setup() |> 
  hc_yAxis(
    min = 0,
    max = 100,
    endOnTick = FALSE,
    labels = list(format = "{value:,.0f}%")
  )
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("
:::
")
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("## Unsolved violent crime by county in", state_name_text)
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz}
total_agencies <- comma(n_distinct(srs_unsolved_by_agency_to_table$ori))

counties_represented <- comma(n_distinct(srs_unsolved_by_agency_to_table$county_clean))
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz}
#| fig-height: 5.5

### choose blues from csgjcr for now
blue1 <- "#d4e9f8"
blue2 <- "#9ed4ef"
blue4 <- "#007392"
blue5 <- "#00475d"

### add tooltip
srs_unsolved_by_county_to_map_tooltip <- srs_unsolved_by_county_to_map |> 
  ### removing counties' unsolved rate if they have fewer than 25 incidents reported in 2022
  ### we want them grayed out on map
  mutate(unsolved_rate_2023 = ifelse(n_reported_2023<25,
                                     NA,unsolved_rate_2023)) |> 
  ### recode any unsolved counts or rates less than 0 to 0
  mutate(unsolved_rate_2023 = ifelse(unsolved_rate_2023<0 & !is.na(unsolved_rate_2023),
                                     0,unsolved_rate_2023),
         n_unsolved_2023 = ifelse(n_unsolved_2023<0 & !is.na(n_unsolved_2023),
                                     0,n_unsolved_2023)) |>
  # ### recode NaN and Inf values as NA
  # mutate(across(.cols = unsolved_rate_percent_point_change_2019_2022, 
  #               ~ ifelse(is.infinite(.x) | is.nan(.x), NA, .x))) |> 
  # ### add '+" in front of positive numbers and 0
  # ### only for value we'll include in tooltip
  mutate(tooltip = case_when(
    n_reported_2023>=25 & !is.na(n_reported_2023) ~ paste0(
    "<b>", county_clean, " – 2023", "</b><br>",
      "Unsolved rate: ", comma(unsolved_rate_2023, 1),"%", "<br>",
      "Violent incidents reported: ", comma(n_reported_2023, 1), "<br>",
      "Violent incidents unsolved: ", comma(n_unsolved_2023, 1), "<br>",
      "Agencies reporting data: ", comma(n_agencies, 1), "<br>",
      "Population covered by reporting agencies: ", comma(nibrs_reported_pop_coverage_pct,1), "%", "<br>"
      ),
    n_reported_2023<25 & !is.na(n_reported_2023) ~ paste0(
      "Agencies in ", county_clean, " did not report enough", "<br>",
      "data on unsolved crime to the FBI to", "<br>",
      "make reliable estimates (fewer than 25 incidents)."
      ),
    is.na(n_reported_2023)==TRUE ~ paste0(
      "No agencies in ", county_clean, " reported data", "<br>",
      "on unsolved crime to the FBI."
      )
    )
    ) 

### extract state abbr for hc map pull
state_abbr <- tolower(head(srs_unsolved_by_county_to_map_tooltip$state_abbr[!is.na(srs_unsolved_by_county_to_map_tooltip$state_abbr)],1))


### pull only state geo that we want for each param iteration
hcmap(
  paste0("countries/us/us-",state_abbr,"-all"),
  data = srs_unsolved_by_county_to_map_tooltip,
  value = "unsolved_rate_2023",
  joinBy = c("fips","county_fips"),
  nullColor = "#E8E8E8",
  nullInteraction = TRUE,
  borderWidth = 0.45,
  borderColor = "black",
  accessibility = list(
      point = list(
        valueDescriptionFormat = "county: {point.acs_county_name}, unsolved rate: {point.value:.1f}"
        )
      )) |> 
  hc_title(
    text = "Unsolved violent crime rate, 2023",
    align = "left",
    style = list(fontFamily = header_font, color = "#005FAD",
                              fontSize = "24px", fontWeight = "bold")
    ) |>   
  hc_colorAxis(endOnTick = TRUE,
               startOnTick = TRUE,
               stops = color_stops(4, c(blue1,
                                        blue2,
                                        blue4,
                                        blue5)),
               labels = list(format = "{value}%",
                             style = list(fontSize = "12px",
                                          fontFamily = default_fonts))) |> 
  hc_legend(align = "center",
            horizontalAlign = "bottom",
            layout = "horizontal",
            # title = list(text = "Percent Change"),
            symbolHeight = 25,
            symbolWidth = 315,
            x = 0,
            y = 0
            ) |> 
     hc_tooltip(
        style = list(fontFamily = default_fonts),
        formatter = JS("function() {
        return '<div style=\"background-color: #FFFFFF; opacity: 1; padding: 8px; border: none;\">' +
        this.point.tooltip +
        '</div>';}"),
        useHTML = TRUE
      )  |>
  hc_setup()

```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat("## Unsolved violent crime by agency in", state_name_text)
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz, results='asis'}
cat(paste0(
  "In the following table, violent crime incident rates, as well as unsolved violent crime rates, are included for each of the ", total_agencies, " law enforcement agencies that reported data in ",state_name_text," in 2023. Please note that rates calculated for agencies with smaller jurisdiction populations, as well as fewer overall incidents, are less reliable than those for larger jurisdictions and should be interpreted with caution."))
```

```{r, eval=!state %in% states_to_exclude_from_unsolved_rate_viz}
srs_unsolved_by_agency_to_table |> 
  ### overwrite rate  with NA if agency is missing population (current value is .inf)
  mutate(n_reported_rate = ifelse(population==0,
                                  NA,n_reported_rate)) |>
  ### remove state name
  ### we just needed it to filter on param
  select(-c(state_name, 
            agency_type,
            number_of_months_missing,
            number_of_months_reported,
            ori)) |> 
  arrange(desc(population)) |>
  reactable_template(
    sort_col = list("population" = "desc"),
    columns = list(
      agency_name = colDef(name = "Agency", minWidth = 250),
      # agency_type = colDef(name = "Agency Type"),
      county_clean = colDef(name = "County", minWidth = 108),
      population = colDef(name = "Jurisdiction Population", minWidth = 116), 
      n_reported = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents Reported (2023)"), 
      n_reported_rate = colDef(name = "Violent Incidents per 100k (2023)",
                               format = colFormat(digits = 0, separators = TRUE)), 
      unsolved_rate = colDef(
        name = "Unsolved Rate (2023)",
        format = colFormat(digits = 0, percent = TRUE)
        )
      )
    )

```


::: {.callout-note icon=false collapse=true}

## Data sources and methodology

Data regarding crime and unsolved rates come from the FBI's [Uniform Crime Reporting Program](https://www.fbi.gov/how-we-can-help-you/need-an-fbi-service-or-more-information/ucr){target="_parent"}. Counts of reported violent crime between 2013 and 2023 are derived from the Summary Reporting System (SRS) and the National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). The FBI generated national estimates for reported crime and clearances based on agency-level reports, and these estimates were published through 2023 in the FBI's annual report, [_Crime in the U.S._](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019){target="_parent"} and on the FBI's [Crime Data Explorer](https://cde.ucr.cjis.gov/LATEST/webapp/#/pages/home){target="_parent"}. For agency-level clearance data, we used [Jacob Kaplan's version of the Offenses Known and Clearances by Arrest](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/OESSD1) dataset.

The FBI stopped SRS data collection after data year 2020 and required law enforcement agencies to use NIBRS to report crime statistics to the FBI. This change decreased the number of agencies that submitted their crime data and thus reduced the reliability of state estimates in 2021. In 2022, the FBI allowed agencies who were unable to submit NIBRS data to submit SRS data again. Ninety-four percent of the U.S. population was covered in the 2023 crime data submissions to the FBI, with most states providing close to complete data. However, the population upon which the estimates were derived decreased by over 10 percent in three states: Florida, Hawaii, and Illinois, so conclusions about crime changes in those states should be interpreted with additional caution.

Crime rates are calculated by dividing the number of estimated incidents by the total U.S. population. Population data come from the U.S. Census Bureau [American Community Survey 1-year estimates (table B01003)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/data.html){target="_parent"} and the [2020 Decennial Census Redistricting Data (table P1)](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/decennial-census/about/rdo/summary-files.html){target="_parent"}.

Unsolved rates are the number of reported crimes not cleared by arrest or by exceptional means divided by the number of reported crimes. A reported crime incident is [cleared by exceptional means](https://ucr.fbi.gov/crime-in-the-u.s/2019/crime-in-the-u.s.-2019/topic-pages/clearances){target='_parent'} if a law enforcement agency is unable to arrest or formally charge a person with a crime, but the agency has met the following conditions:
    
a) Identified the person who committed the crime
b) Gathered enough evidence to support an arrest, make a charge, and turn over the person to the court for prosecution
c) Identified the suspect’s exact location so that the person could be taken into custody immediately
d) Encountered a circumstance outside the control of law enforcement that prohibits the agency from arresting, charging, and prosecuting the person
    
Examples of exceptional clearances include the death of the person who committed the crime; the victim’s refusal to cooperate with the prosecution after the person who committed the crime has been identified; or the denial of extradition.

In some instances, the unsolved rate and number of unsolved crimes may be less than 0. This is due to the fact that the number of reported crimes cleared in a year can include incidents reported in prior years. For this reason, it is possible to have more cleared crimes than new reported incidents in a given year.

Agency- and county-level violent crime and unsolved rate data presented above are restricted to agencies reporting data for all 12 months in 2023. The calculation of population covered by reporting agencies, as well as the number of reporting agencies by county, is also based on agencies with violent crime data for all 12 months. 

Each law enforcement agency is grouped by the county where they are based using the Law Enforcement Agency Identifiers Crosswalk developed by the Bureau of Justice Statistics in 2012. The overall county population used for calculation of county-level rates are based on the sum of the jurisdiction populations for agencies reporting data for all 12 months in 2023.

Counties with fewer than 25 reported violent incidents in 2023 are excluded from the map, as calculations of unsolved rates are not reliable. 

:::
